title: L-CITRULLINE
description: L-Citrulline is a non-essential amino acid that converts to L-arginine in the body, increasing nitric oxide (NO) production through both NOS-dependent and NOS-independent pathways. It enhances blood flow, vascular function, and exercise performance by promoting vasodilation, and is more effective than direct L-arginine supplementation due to superior bioavailability and absorption.

research.title: Effects of Citrulline Supplementation on Exercise Performance in Humans: A Review of the Current Literature
research.summary: Comprehensive review examining L-citrulline's ergogenic properties for both aerobic and anaerobic exercise performance through enhanced L-arginine bioavailability and nitric oxide production.

research.title: l-Citrulline Supplementation: Impact on Cardiometabolic Health
research.summary: L-citrulline supplementation improves nitric oxide bioavailability more effectively than L-arginine, with beneficial impacts on cardiovascular and metabolic health markers.

research.title: Supplementation with Nitric Oxide Precursors for Strength Performance: A Review of the Current Literature
research.summary: L-citrulline serves as an effective precursor to L-arginine and promotes nitric oxide production, potentially improving blood flow and mechanisms contributing to skeletal muscle performance and hypertrophy.

research.title: Dietary Arginine and Citrulline Supplements for Cardiovascular Health and Athletic Performance: A Narrative Review
research.summary: Review of L-citrulline and citrulline malate supplements showing potential effects on hemodynamic function, endothelial function, aerobic and anaerobic capacity, strength, power, and endurance.

research.title: A critical review of citrulline malate supplementation and exercise performance
research.summary: Citrulline malate may enhance exercise performance through improved blood flow, ammonia homeostasis, or increased ATP production via greater malate availability, with potential muscle recovery benefits.

research.title: Effect of food sources of nitrate, polyphenols, L-arginine and L-citrulline on endurance exercise performance: a systematic review and meta-analysis
research.summary: Systematic review and meta-analysis evaluating foods containing L-citrulline and other nitric oxide-promoting compounds on endurance exercise performance outcomes.

research.title: L-Citrulline Supplementation and Exercise in the Management of Sarcopenia
research.summary: Examines L-citrulline supplementation combined with exercise as a strategy for managing sarcopenia and age-related muscle loss.

research.title: Side effects of amino acid supplements
research.summary: Comprehensive examination of side effects from amino acid supplementation including citrulline, noting potential impacts on renal and gastrointestinal function, ammonia production, and nutrient competition.

sentiment.summary: Users consistently report positive effects on erectile function, vascularity, workout performance, and overall blood flow. Most consider it effective, affordable, and well-tolerated with minimal side effects, though some note it requires proper dosing (3-8g daily) and may cause GI upset in sensitive individuals.

effects.label: Dosage-Dependent:
effects.description: Effects are most noticeable at 3-8g daily doses; lower doses (500-1000mg) often insufficient for significant benefits

effects.label: Time to Effect:
effects.description: Most users notice blood flow improvements within 24-48 hours, with erectile benefits often appearing as next-day morning wood; cognitive effects may take 1-2 weeks

effects.label: Individual Variation:
effects.description: Highly effective for those with circulation issues or erectile dysfunction; those with already optimal blood flow may notice minimal effects

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Many users report enhanced effects when combined with other nitric oxide boosters, though some find citrulline alone sufficient

dosage.label: Standard Dose:
dosage.description: 3-6 grams daily is most commonly recommended and reported effective for vasodilation and performance benefits

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Can be taken pre-workout for performance or daily for consistent blood flow benefits; some users split doses (morning and evening)

dosage.label: Powder vs Capsules:
dosage.description: Bulk powder form preferred for cost-effectiveness and ability to achieve higher therapeutic doses (capsules often underdosed at 500-750mg)

dosage.label: Citrulline Malate:
dosage.description: Some users prefer citrulline malate (2:1 ratio) for additional ATP production benefits, though pure L-citrulline is also effective

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Issues:
sideEffects.description: Most common side effect is stomach upset, bloating, or diarrhea, especially at higher doses or when combined with other supplements

sideEffects.label: Hypotension:
sideEffects.description: Excessive vasodilation can cause dizziness, lightheadedness, or headaches in some users, particularly when combined with other vasodilators

sideEffects.label: Viral Reactivation:
sideEffects.description: Important warning that arginine/citrulline can fuel virus replication (HSV, shingles); users with herpes history should balance with lysine supplementation

sideEffects.label: Minimal Long-Term Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Users report safe long-term use for years when properly dosed, with no tolerance build-up or serious adverse effects

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Easily sourced from major supplement retailersand online retailers; bulk powder is most cost-effective at approximately $0.10-0.30 per serving

availability.label: Quality Variance:
availability.description: Users recommend checking for pure L-citrulline or citrulline malate without excessive fillers; some report pharmaceutical glaze and other additives in tablet forms

availability.label: Pre-Workout Inclusion:
availability.description: Commonly found in pre-workout formulas but often underdosed (1-2g); serious users prefer standalone supplementation for adequate dosing
